title: 3D Scroll, Subgrid, Thermal Text, Ink & Springs
description: Five live UI demos: scroll-driven 3D card entrances, subgrid editorial layout, thermal cursor text, CSS ink splash, and a spring radial menu.

drops.title: Scroll-Driven 3D Entrance
drops.description: Pure CSS cards fly in from unique 3D angles as they scroll into the viewport using animation-timeline: view().
drops.concept: CSS Scroll-Driven Animations let you tie @keyframes progress directly to an element's scroll position — no JS. Each card in this demo uses a different rotateY/rotateX/translateZ combo to tumble in from a distinct direction.

drops.title: CSS Subgrid Editorial Layout
drops.description: Magazine-style 12-column grid using grid-template-rows: subgrid to align baselines across the feature and sidebar cards.
drops.concept: Subgrid allows a child grid item to inherit the row or column tracks of its parent, enabling precise cross-element alignment. This layout uses subgrid on both the feature article and the sidebar so their internal content rows share the same horizontal baselines.
drops.source: MDN CSS Subgrid + Ahmad Shadeed editoral grid patterns

drops.title: Thermal Proximity Text
drops.description: Per-character CSS hsl() transitions from cold blue to white-hot based on cursor distance, updated via a single rAF loop.
drops.concept: Each character is wrapped in a span with a --heat CSS custom property. On mousemove, JS computes distance from cursor to each character's center and updates --heat. CSS hsl() uses that value to shift hue (cold=220°, hot=0°), lightness, and text-shadow intensity — creating a thermal sensor effect.
drops.source: Technique inspired by variable-font proximity effects on Codepen trending

drops.title: CSS Ink Splash Buttons
drops.description: Origin-tracked ripple effect where JS sets --x/--y vars and toggles a class; the entire animation is pure CSS ::after @keyframes.
drops.concept: The ripple pseudo-element is anchored to CSS custom properties --x and --y that JavaScript updates on click to match the exact click position. A forced reflow (void el.offsetWidth) restarts the animation on repeated clicks. The technique keeps all visual logic in CSS while using a tiny JS footprint.
drops.source: CSS ripple origin-tracking pattern (CSS-Tricks / material design)

drops.title: Spring Radial Menu
drops.description: Three radial menus (arc, circle, semicircle) where items spring to position with staggered delays using per-item spring simulations.
drops.concept: A minimalist spring class (stiffness=320, damping=28) drives x, y, and scale for each menu item independently. Opening staggers the target-set by 30ms per item, creating an organic explosion feel. The menu supports arc, full-circle, and semicircle configurations via startAngle + sweep parameters.
drops.source: Spring physics UI pattern from Radix Primitives / Emil Kowalski's spring animations

interaction.mouse: Click + to open, click item or outside to close
